raw.population: N: 100 (hypothetical) no specifications of covariate distribution (age, weight, gender)
raw.objective: “The purpose of this study was to study the effect of delaying, missing and replacing doses on VPA concentrations following both the ER and DR preparations using Monte Carlo simulations.”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, WT, SEX Included: None
raw.observations: Effect of delayed/missed dose on VPA concentration depends on dose and formulation. Non-linear binding of VPA to albumin incorporated in model. Total VPA concentrations show high inter-individual variability. Covariates in model are not specified; a limitation of the simulation is the that the hypothetical population generated by Monte Carlo does not necessarily reflect overall population.
raw.structure: one-compartment model with first-order elimination [delayed release formulation] and zero-order absorption [extended release]. Unbound and total concentration models are given
raw.equations: CL= theta1*exp(eta1) V= theta2*exp(eta2) N1=theta3*(1+eta3) K1=theta4*(1+eta4) N2=theta5*(1+eta5) K2=theta6*(1+eta6) P=theta7*(1+eta7) KA= theta8 F= theta9 ALAG1= 2 #for DR formulation KE=CL/V #DR-BID: Delayed release, twice daily Cu=((KA*DOSE)/(V*(KA-KE)))*(exp(-KE*TIME)-(exp(-KA*TIME))) #ER-QD: Extended release, once daily Cu=((F*DOSE)/(CL*TAU))*((exp(KE*TAU))-1)*(exp(-KE*TIME)) #Total VPA concentration Ct=Cu+((N1*K1*Cu*P)/(1+K1*Cu))+((N2*K2*Cu*P)/(1+K2*Cu)) #no error model reported DOSE: VPA dose in mg Cu: unbound VPA concentration Ct: total VPA concentration Vu: Volume of distribution CLu: systemic clearance of unbound drug F: bioavailability Ka: first-order absorption rate constant for DR T: duration of zero order input for ER TAU: dosing interval in hours P: Albumin concentration N1/N2: number of binding sites K1/K2: equilibrium association constants
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 5.04 theta2= 95.1 theta3=1.54 theta4=0.083 theta5=0.194 theta6=1.14 theta7=35.376 theta8= 0.1 theta9= 0.89 omega1= 0.0395 omega2= 0.0399 omega3= 0.0049 omega4= 0.0289 omega5=0.1629 omega6=0.7399 omega7=0.01

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.implementation: Canonical structure is normalized first; ADVAN/TRANS is then deterministically inferred with provenance and alternatives.
normalization.population: Free-text cohorts and summary statistics are retained and additionally normalized into structured cohort descriptors.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
